sections.paragraph:
an unused root, the true sense of which has been altogether neglected by etymologists. Pr. it is to cut, to cut down boughs, or trees, like the Ethiopic ገመደ፡ and Arab. جَمَدَ Kamûs p. 353, comp. transp. גַּדֵּם, ܓܕܡ. Hence is formed-

(1) גֹּמֶד a branch, a staff, hence a cubit; the same verb is applied to brave warriors who cut down enemies like trees; hence-(2) Arabic جَمَدَ and Syriac Aphel, to be fierce as a soldier. Hence the word which has greatly perplexed interpreters-
